metadata.test_description: Checks for architectural and design weaknesses that lead to security risks.

analysis.summary: The analyzed source code is an Arduino-based IR remote filter application that receives, filters, and retransmits infrared signals for home automation purposes. This is embedded firmware running on a microcontroller with no network connectivity, user authentication, account management, session handling, or any web/API surface. The security concepts outlined in the test instructions (rate limiting, password reset, account lockout, authentication, remember-me tokens, trust boundaries for client-server) are entirely inapplicable to this type of embedded IR relay device. No insecure design vulnerabilities relevant to the provided test criteria were identified.
